req.title: Manager updates department
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to update a department through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: This suite validates the manager department update API endpoint across the full decision space: successful updates with audit trail (positive), authentication/authorization failures and validation errors (negative), and field boundary constraints (boundary). Tests use Equivalence Partitioning to cover valid/invalid input classes, Boundary Value Analysis for string length limits, and Decision Table logic to verify the auth/role decision matrix. All tests are self-contained, deterministic, and idempotent via unique test data and isolation.

tests.name: test_manager_update_department_valid_all_fields
preconditions: manager authenticated with valid bearer token
department ID D-001 exists with current state {name: 'Sales', budget: 50000}
actions: POST /api/departments/D-001 with payload {name: 'Sales Revised', budget: 75000, manager_id: 'MGR-123'}
expected: audit record exists with action:update, actor:current_user, resource:D-001, timestamp within last 5 seconds
tests.executable:
def test_manager_update_department_valid_all_fields():
    auth_header = <get_manager_bearer_token>
    dept_id = <create_test_department>
    payload = {
        'name': 'Sales Revised',
        'budget': 75000,
        'manager_id': 'MGR-123'
    }
    resp = <post_request>(
        f'/api/departments/{dept_id}',
        json=payload,
        headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {auth_header}'}
    )
    assert resp.status_code in [200, 201]
    assert resp.json()['id'] == dept_id
    assert resp.json()['name'] == 'Sales Revised'
    assert resp.json()['budget'] == 75000
    audit = <query_audit_log>(resource_id=dept_id)
    assert audit[-1]['action'] == 'update'
    assert audit[-1]['actor'] == <current_user_id>

tests.name: test_manager_update_department_missing_mandatory_field
preconditions: manager authenticated with valid bearer token
name is a mandatory field for update
actions: extract response status and error message
expected: no audit record written for this request

tests.name: test_manager_update_department_unauthenticated
actions: POST /api/departments/D-003 with valid payload but no Authorization header
expected: response contains error code: UNAUTHORIZED or AUTH_REQUIRED

tests.name: test_manager_update_department_insufficient_role
preconditions: user authenticated as employee (role:employee, not role:manager)
actions: POST /api/departments/D-004 with valid payload and employee bearer token
expected: response contains error code: FORBIDDEN or INSUFFICIENT_ROLE

tests.name: test_manager_update_department_name_boundary_max_length
preconditions: department name has max length constraint of 255 characters
actions: POST /api/departments/D-005 with name field set to exactly 255 characters
extract response status and updated name
expected: response body contains name with exactly 255 characters
tests.executable:
def test_manager_update_department_name_boundary_max_length():
    auth_header = <get_manager_bearer_token>
    dept_id = <create_test_department>
    max_name = 'A' * 255
    payload = {'name': max_name, 'budget': 40000}
    resp = <post_request>(
        f'/api/departments/{dept_id}',
        json=payload,
        headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {auth_header}'}
    )
    assert resp.status_code in [200, 201]
    assert len(resp.json()['name']) == 255
    assert resp.json()['name'] == max_name
    audit = <query_audit_log>(resource_id=dept_id)
    assert audit[-1]['action'] == 'update'

tests.name: test_manager_update_department_name_exceeds_max_length
preconditions: department name has max length constraint of 255 characters
actions: POST /api/departments/D-006 with name field set to 256 characters
expected: response message references length or max constraint

tests.name: test_manager_update_department_budget_negative_value
preconditions: budget field has constraint: must be >= 0
expected: response message references negative value or minimum constraint
tests.executable:
def test_manager_update_department_budget_negative_value():
    auth_header = <get_manager_bearer_token>
    dept_id = <create_test_department>
    orig_budget = <get_department>(dept_id)['budget']
    payload = {'name': 'Finance', 'budget': -1000}
    resp = <post_request>(
        f'/api/departments/{dept_id}',
        json=payload,
        headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {auth_header}'}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 400
    body = resp.json()
    assert body['error_code'] == 'VALIDATION_ERROR'
    assert 'budget' in str(body)
    dept = <get_department>(dept_id)
    assert dept['budget'] == orig_budget
